title: N digit numbers
hints: Use dynamic programming with a 2D table where dp[digits][sum] represents the number of ways to form a number with 'digits' digits that sum to 'sum'.
For 1-digit numbers, the first digit must be 1-9 (no leading zeros). So dp[1][d] = 1 for d from 1 to 9.
For numbers with more than 1 digit, you can use digits 0-9 for positions after the first digit.
For dp[digits][sum], try all possible digits d (0-9) and add dp[digits-1][sum-d] if sum-d >= 0.
Remember to take modulo 1000000007 at each step to prevent integer overflow.
